design_philosophy.core_loop: Destroy enemy bases in top-view stages, then survive horizontal scrolling combat with weapon switching and defensive positioning
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (weapon loadout) balanced against incoming enemy patterns; trade-off between offensive power and defensive coverage via CRAW pods
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot an advanced fighter jet (FIRE LEO-02 Exceliza) against an overwhelming imperial armada; progressive tactical escalation through varied weaponry
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid stage structure (top-view base-destruction + side-view scrolling) creates two distinct gameplay contexts within one stage. Weapon loss on death enforces moment-to-moment caution. CRAW defensive mechanics reward spatial positioning. Series-defining weapon system established here becomes franchiseTemplate

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with ship destruction resetting weapon loadout to defaults
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpoints; top-view and side-view sections can be retried independently after failure
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through weapon selection and CRAW management. Player can switch between obtained weapons at will to adapt to incoming threats. Choosing to equip CRAWs trades firepower for bullet defense.

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon collection and upgrade system. Default weapons (Twin shot, Back shot, bomb) can be powered up via item drops. New weapons (Hunter, others) obtained through collection; each stage maintains separate weapon pools (top-view vs side-view). CRAW pods stack up to two units.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression gates on base destruction count in top-view stages before side-view unlock. Weapon access gated by item drops during play.
replayability_hooks: Multiple weapon options create different playstyle approaches per stage
Top-view base routing optimization (player chooses destruction order)

world_building_techniques: Fictional Galaxy Federation vs ORN Empire conflict establishes stakes
Named antagonist battleship (Plealos/Preareos) gives concrete objective
Player ship naming (FIRE LEO-02 Exceliza) ties player to faction identity

boss_design.philosophy: End-of-stage 'huge end-of-level guardians' serve as climactic encounters. Bosses appear after completing top-view base destruction objective, signaling stage progression.
notable_bosses: Plealos (final objective battleship - implied end-game encounter)

faction_indicators: Galaxy Federation (player faction) - controls FIRE LEO series fighters
ORN Empire (antagonist) - controls Plealos battleship and Nepura occupation forces
danger_signals: Destruction of planet Reda by ORN
Plealos battleship deployment as existential threat
environmental_storytelling: Stages take place on ORN-occupied or contested worlds
Base structures in top-view stages represent enemy infrastructure
Enemy ship density and type reflects ORN military capability in each region
